TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavCon-banana trees_abas_ipay2
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: XXXX temawaR si a anu qumni yau dawadsu si sanisika ya peninaku yau peninaku mwaza.
S.PHON: **** təmawaʁ si a anu qumni jau ɮawaɮsu si sanisika ja pəninaku jau pəninaku mwaz~ra.
S.FORM: XXXX temawaR si a anu qumni yau dawadsu si sanisika ya peninaku yau peninaku mwaza.
S.PHON: **** təmawaʁ si a anu qumni jau ɮawaɮsu si sanisika ja pəninaku jau pəninaku mwaz~ra.
S.TRANSL: 如果你明天有空的話,來削我的香蕉樹皮.我有很多香蕉樹皮.
If you have time tomorrow, bark my banana trees. I have much banana bark.

S.FORM: XXXX XXXX nengi nayaupaita XXXX nayawpaita.
XXXX XXXX nengi nayaupaita XXXX nayawpaita.

TRANSL.notes: 我說讓你削樹皮,(你要做也可以不要做也可以).
'Bark (the banana trees),' said I. (It's up to you.)

S.TRANSL: Yes, good. You will also do so.

TRANSL.notes: It would be better (if we wait till) the athletic meet is over.

S.TRANSL: It would be better the athletic meet is over.

S.FORM: XXXX naquni si yau dawadsu si sanisika ya peninaku haw.
S.PHON: **** naquni si jau ɮawaɮsu si sanisika ja pəninaku haw.
S.FORM: XXXX naquni si yau dawadsu si sanisika ya peninaku haw.
S.PHON: **** naquni si jau ɮawaɮsu si sanisika ja pəninaku haw.
S.TRANSL: 這樣的話,你有空的話,來削我的香蕉樹皮.
If so, if you have time, bark my banana trees.
嗯,那我們就那樣做,我跟你說,'削樹皮'.
Okay, let's do so. 'Bark banana trees,' I said to you.
You have to respond to me in this way.
沒有了嗎?還有沒有?介紹給你.
Finished? Anything else? Let me introduce to you.

TRANSL.notes: 你開始(削香蕉樹皮)了嗎?
Have you started (barking the banana trees)?
還沒,還沒有開始(削香蕉樹皮).
Not yet. (I) haven't begun (barking the banana trees).
